summary: Iteration 3 regression pass — all 4 refactors verified. Backend pytest 25/25 pass (~17s) with lifespan API; backend logs show only clean 'Application startup complete' with ZERO on_event deprecation warnings. Health, auth (login OK/wrong-pass 401/me OK/me 401), Products/Offers/Announcements CRUD + active_only filter + auth guards + seed verification (6 products incl. A55 5G, 4 announcements incl. Fold8 pre-reserve, 3 offers incl. Fold8/S25/A-series) all green. Frontend Playwright: home SSR renders all 10 sections (hero, announcement-strip, fold8-banner, features, offers-section, products-section, visit, cta, footer, whatsapp-fab) + 6 product-cards + 3 offer-cards. Admin refactor: dashboard shell + all 3 tabs (Products/Offers/Announcements) load their data on switch. AlertDialog integration verified end-to-end on all 3 resources: 5 test IDs present (alert-dialog, -backdrop, -close, -cancel, -confirm); cancel button does nothing; ESC closes; backdrop click closes; confirm fires actual DELETE and card is removed; ENTER key also confirms. Full create+edit+toggle+delete via dialog verified for products, offers, announcements. GA4 no-op verified: 0 googletagmanager/gtag scripts and 0 google-site-verification meta in home HTML (both env vars empty). SEO intact: title + description + JSON-LD ElectronicsStore (2 stores → 4 schema hits) + robots.txt (Disallow /admin + /admin/*) + sitemap.xml (200, contains /about /contact /offers /stores, no /admin).
critical_code_review_comments: Lifespan migration is correct: @asynccontextmanager lifespan(_: FastAPI) yields between startup (indexes + admin seed + _seed_defaults) and shutdown (client.close). FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan) wires it. No on_event calls remain — deprecation warning gone.

AlertDialog uses `useEffect` with a window keydown listener + body scroll lock. Cleanup restores prior overflow — clean. Enter=confirm, Escape=cancel, backdrop onClick=cancel, close X=cancel. All 5 test IDs (alert-dialog, -backdrop, -close, -cancel, -confirm) present.

useConfirm() hook resolves a Promise on confirm/cancel — panels use `if (!(await confirm(...))) return;` which is idiomatic. Only pitfall: the hook holds state via `useState`; if the parent unmounts while dialog is open, resolver is dropped. Not a real bug for admin panels (they persist).

Admin page.jsx split down to 133 lines (from 595). ProductsPanel/OffersPanel/AnnouncementsPanel each own their own useConfirm + dialog render. shared.jsx supplies PanelHead/FormPanel/FormRow/IconBtn/StatusPill/EmptyState/ActiveToggle. Good separation.

Analytics.jsx correctly returns null when NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID is empty — confirmed 0 gtag scripts in home HTML. metadata.verification.google is `undefined` when GOOGLE_SITE_VERIFICATION empty — confirmed 0 <meta name="google-site-verification"> in HTML.

CARRIED-OVER (still not fixed, non-blocking): CORS_ORIGINS='*' + allow_credentials=True is still spec-non-compliant; harmless today because auth uses Bearer header, not cookies. Should be tightened before switching to cookie auth.
CARRIED-OVER: CORS middleware still added AFTER app.include_router(router). Works fine but convention is middleware first.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all admin CRUD + AlertDialog + home + SEO checks passed)

test_credentials: admin@digiconnect.net.in / DigiConnect@2026 — verified. JWT in localStorage key dc_admin_token.
seed_data_creation: None persisted. TEST_dlg_prod, TEST_enter_prod, TEST_dlg_offer(+edited), TEST_dlg_ann(+edited) were all created and deleted in-run via the AlertDialog flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` (25 tests, ~17s, parallel via pytest-xdist). AlertDialog test IDs on admin: alert-dialog, alert-dialog-backdrop, alert-dialog-close, alert-dialog-cancel, alert-dialog-confirm. Delete buttons on cards: delete-product-{id}, delete-offer-{id}, delete-ann-{id} — they now OPEN the dialog (not window.confirm). Do NOT attach `page.on('dialog', ...)` for these — click alert-dialog-confirm instead. Login gotcha: after page.click on admin-login-submit, do NOT use page.expect_response as a wrapper if navigation is fast — the Response event fires before the wrapper attaches. Simpler: click, then wait_for_selector('[data-testid="tab-products"]'). GA4/site-verification are no-ops while NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID='' and GOOGLE_SITE_VERIFICATION='' — verify via HTML source (0 matches for googletagmanager/gtag/google-site-verification).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. One Playwright authoring gotcha discovered: using `async with page.expect_response(...)` as a wrapper around click can race with fast redirects; simpler pattern is click then wait_for_selector on the post-nav element. Updated test approach accordingly.
